svg.title: Fourier option pricer workflow
svg.desc: Six-stage pipeline: three models expose characteristic functions through a unified CharFunc protocol, feed three Fourier pricers, produce prices that are converted to implied vols, pass a validation gate against published tables, then flow into benchmarks. Monte Carlo sits alongside as a baseline comparison.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the Heston characteristic function and the branch-cut trap')
sendPrompt('Explain the Variance Gamma characteristic function')
sendPrompt('Explain the Kou jump-diffusion characteristic function')
sendPrompt('What is the CharFunc protocol and why unify pricers behind it')
sendPrompt('How does Carr-Madan FFT pricing work and where does it break')
sendPrompt('How does FRFT decouple the grids compared to plain FFT')
sendPrompt('Explain the COS method and the cumulant truncation rule')
sendPrompt('How do you robustly invert prices to Black-Scholes implied vols')
sendPrompt('Why is reproducing published reference tables required before any speed claim')
sendPrompt('What benchmarks should this repo report and in what order')
sendPrompt('What role does Monte Carlo play as a baseline in this repo')
